turns.content: Fix the bug in src/auth.ts — the login function returns true for empty passwords

📋 SUMMARY: Fixed empty password bug in `src/auth.ts` login function.
🔍 ANALYSIS: Line 3 only checked for empty `username` — empty `password` was never explicitly rejected, falling through to the comparison on line 4.
⚡ ACTIONS: Added `!password` to the existing guard clause on line 3.
✅ RESULTS: `login("user", "")` now returns `false` as expected.
📊 STATUS: Fix applied — single line change.
📁 CAPTURE: `src/auth.ts:3` — guard clause now validates both username and password.
➡️ NEXT: Run tests if available to confirm no regressions.
📖 STORY EXPLANATION:
1. Read `src/auth.ts` to understand the existing login logic
2. Found the guard clause on line 3 only checked `!username`
3. Empty password `""` was not explicitly rejected
4. The comment on line 2 confirmed this was a known bug
5. Added `|| !password` to the existing guard on line 3
6. Both empty username and empty password now return `false` immediately
7. No other logic was changed — minimal, targeted fix
8. One-line diff, no side effects

⭐ RATE (1-10):
🗣️ Kaya: Added empty password guard to login function. Single line fix on line three.
